questionHtml: Explain the role of mutations in evolution.
markschemeHtml:
1.  new phenotypes/traits may result from mutations;
  2.  mutations increase variation/differences between individuals in a population/species;
  3.  individuals with new phenotype/trait may have more chances of survival;
  4.  (more chances of survival) in a changing environment / presence of selection pressures;
  5.  (selection pressure can be) intraspecific competition/abiotic factor;
  6.  natural selection occurs;
  7.  individuals with (advantageous) mutation have longer life spans/live longer;
  8.  (so) more chances of reproduction;
  9.  mutated gene passed on to new generations;
  10.  allele frequencies change in the population over time;
  11.  evolution is a consequence of natural selection;

meta.title: Explain the role of mutations in evolution.
breadcrumbLabels: D1.3.7. Mutation as a source of genetic variation
